source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $AM$ is the bisector of $\angle CAB$, and $CN$ is the bisector of the exterior angle $\angle GCB$. $BE \perp AM$ at point E, and $BD \perp CN$ at point D, with $DE$ connected. If $AB=4$, $BC=5$, and $AC=6$, what is the length of $DE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, \\
extend $BE$ to meet $AC$ at point $F$, and extend $AC$ and $BD$ to meet at point $G$,\\
since $AM$ bisects $\angle CAB$ and $BE\perp AM$,\\
it follows that $\angle CAM=\angle BAM$, $\angle AEF=\angle AEB=90^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle AFE=90^\circ-\angle CAM$, $\angle ABE=90^\circ-\angle BAM$,\\
hence $\angle AFE=\angle ABE$,\\
which implies $AF=AB$,\\
given that $AB=4$, $AC=6$, and $BC=5$,\\
it follows that $FC=AC-AF=AC-AB=6-4=2$,\\
since $CN$ bisects $\angle GCB$ and $BD\perp CN$,\\
it follows that $\angle GCN=\angle BCN$, $\angle CDB=\angle CDG=90^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle CGD=90^\circ-\angle GCN$, $\angle CBD=90^\circ-\angle BCN$,\\
hence $\angle CGD=\angle CBD$,\\
which implies $CG=CB=5$,\\
therefore $FG=FC+CG=2+5=7$,\\
since $AF=AB$ and $BE\perp AM$,\\
it follows that $AE$ is the median to side $BF$, that is, point $E$ is the midpoint of $BF$,\\
since $CG=CB$ and $BD\perp CN$,\\
it follows that $CD$ is the median to side $BG$, that is, point $D$ is the midpoint of $BG$,\\
thus $DE$ is the midsegment of $\triangle BFG$,\\
therefore $DE=\frac{1}{2}FG=\boxed{\frac{7}{2}}$.

Since $CN$ bisects $\angle GCB$ and $BD\perp CN$, it follows that $\angle GCN=\angle BCN$, $\angle CDB=\angle CDG=90^\circ$. Thus $\angle CGD=90^\circ-\angle GCN$, $\angle CBD=90^\circ-\angle BCN$, hence $\angle CGD=\angle CBD$, which implies $CG=CB=5$. Therefore $FG=FC+CG=2+5=7$. Since $AF=AB$ and $BE\perp AM$, it follows that $AE$ is the median to side $BF$, that is, point $E$ is the midpoint of $BF$. Since $CG=CB$ and $BD\perp CN$, it follows that $CD$ is the median to side $BG$, that is, point $D$ is the midpoint of $BG$. Thus $DE$ is the midsegment of $\triangle BFG$, therefore $DE=\frac{1}{2}FG=\boxed{\frac{7}{2}}$.

If $AB=4$, $BC=5$, and $AC=6$, what is the length of $DE$?
variants.expected_answer: \textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, extend $BE$ to meet $AC$ at point $F$, and extend $AC$ and $BD$ to meet at point $G$. Given that $AB=4$, $AC=6$, and $BC=5$, it follows that $FC=AC-AF=AC-AB=6-4=2$. Since $CN$ bisects $\angle GCB$ and $BD\perp CN$, it follows that $\angle GCN=\angle BCN$, $\angle CDB=\angle CDG=90^\circ$. Thus $DE$ is the midsegment of $\triangle BFG$, therefore $DE=\frac{1}{2}FG=\boxed{\frac{7}{2}}$.
